_comment: Policy YOU (the human) own. It records how the offload worker is allowed to act. WRITES are still mediated: the worker holds no write/bash tools and must emit capability-request{write} for the ORCHESTRATOR (cloud Claude) to fulfill per this file. WEB is now DIRECT: the on-device model calls web_search/web_fetch itself, bounded by tool-level guards + a sandbox (see below), not by the orchestrator. The worker cannot edit this file to widen its own privileges (deny_paths + sandbox). Decisions: allow | ask | deny | direct.

write._note: Still ORCHESTRATOR-mediated (the worker has no write tool). Writes under sandbox/ are auto-approved; elsewhere the orchestrator asks (y/N). deny_paths are NEVER written on the worker's behalf — they protect the gate's own guardrails, including tools/ (the web_fetch SSRF guard).

web_search._note: The on-device model runs web_search itself via aichat (Tavily). No orchestrator query-screening anymore. Residual exfiltration channel: the QUERY STRING (and the prompt the orchestrator hands the model). Mitigation: the sandbox denies the model read-access to files outside the per-task roots (see read_confinement), so there is little ambient secret to fold into a query; keep task prompts free of secrets.

guarantees.reject_private_ip: loopback, link-local (incl. 169.254.169.254), private, reserved, multicast, unspecified, ipv4-mapped

web_fetch._note: Was DEFERRED/deny; now ENABLED as a hardened direct tool. Arbitrary-URL fetch is the strongest exfil/SSRF channel, so it is allowed ONLY through tools/web_fetch.sh: host must be on tools/fetch-allowlist.txt, the resolved IP must be public (SSRF guard in tools/url_guard.py), curl is pinned to that IP (no DNS-rebinding) and does not follow redirects. Never enable fetch_url_via_curl/jina in aichat-config — those are UNguarded.

read_confinement.policy: allow-default, then deny file-read-data under $HOME, re-allowing only aichat-config/, the functions build, tools/, caches, and the per-task roots (-f files + --read-root dirs)
read_confinement._note: This is what makes direct web safe: the web-capable model cannot READ the user's private files (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/Library, ~/Code source) to exfiltrate them. run-local.sh refuses to run unconfined unless --no-sandbox is passed explicitly.

bash._note: The model has only read-only fs (fs_ls, fs_cat) + guarded web. Do not grant shell on its behalf.
